qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated SSRF in admin/install.php via user-controlled path parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in MantisBT's admin/install.php via a user-controlled 'path' parameter. The advisory describes stored HTML injection/XSS in tag_delete.php via improper escaping of tag names. While both affect MantisBT, they are different vulnerable files (admin/install.php vs tag_delete.php), different root causes (unvalidated URL fetching vs missing output encoding), different vulnerability classes (SSRF vs XSS), and different attack surfaces. No shared vulnerable code or equivalent source-to-sink condition.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes stored HTML injection/XSS in MantisBT's tag_delete.php through improper escaping of tag names (CVE-2026-33517, patch 80990f43153167c73f11eb4b2bc7108d0c3d6b46). Finding 'ssrf-admin-install' is SSRF in MantisBT's admin/install.php, which is a different vulnerable component, different vulnerability class, and different root cause with no overlap with the tag_delete.php XSS vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-fh48-f69w-7vmp (CVE-2026-33517) identifies tag_delete.php as the vulnerable file with improper escaping of tag names leading to stored HTML injection/XSS.
Finding 'ssrf-admin-install' targets MantisBT's admin/install.php with SSRF via the 'path' parameter, unrelated to tag_delete.php or XSS.
No finding references tag_delete.php, tag name handling, HTML escaping, or XSS in the context of tag deletion confirmation.
